# Afterburn — LLM-Ready Documentation

> Extract residual intelligence from spent Claude Code sessions.
> Repository: https://github.com/zenprocess/afterburn
> License: MIT | Part of standra.ai — Open Standards for the AI-Ready Enterprise

## What Afterburn Does

Afterburn mines Claude Code conversation history (~/.claude/projects/) to:
1. Find recurring friction (corrections, errors, tool denials)
2. Extract successful patterns (confirmed approaches → CLAUDE.md rules)
3. Identify skill gaps (manual workflows → candidate SKILL.md files)
4. Generate narrative reports (what you did, what went well, what didn't)
5. Evolve existing skills via metric-gated experiment loops (CCAR)
6. Archive old sessions to .tgz and clean history

## Architecture

Session JSONL files → Scanner → Analysis Passes → Findings → Reports

Two processing paths based on session size:
- < 10MB: Direct regex extraction (no LLM needed)
- >= 10MB: RLM REPL recursive decomposition (LLM writes Python to chunk and analyze)

Three LLM backends, auto-detected:
1. AFTERBURN_API_URL → OpenAI-compatible API (vLLM, Ollama)
2. claude -p → Claude Code CLI pipe mode (zero config)
3. localhost:8080 fallback

## Session Data Format

Claude Code stores conversations as JSONL at ~/.claude/projects/<project-slug>/*.jsonl
Each line is a JSON object:
- type: "user" | "assistant" | "file-history-snapshot"
- message.role: "user" | "assistant"
- message.content: string or array of blocks (text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result)
- sessionId, cwd, version, gitBranch

Worktree sessions (slug contains "worktree") are excluded by default.

## CLI Commands

```
afterburn discover [--pass friction|patterns|gaps|releases] [--since DATE] [--project SLUG] [--projects A,B,C] [--project-group /path] [--format json] [--max-calls N]
afterburn narrative [--today|--week|--month|--since DATE] [--project SLUG] [--projects A,B,C] [--project-group /path] [--no-llm]
afterburn evolve --skill NAME [--max-iterations N] [--dry-run]
afterburn archive [--days N] [--dry-run]
afterburn status
afterburn install [--global]
```

## Claude Code Slash Commands

After `afterburn install`:
- /enhanced-insights — Narrative report with selectable timeframe
- /afterburn-discover — Deep 3-pass analysis
- /afterburn-evolve — CCAR skill evolution
- /afterburn-archive — Archive and cleanup
- /afterburn-status — Last run summary

## Python API

### Scanner

```python
from afterburn.scanner import discover_sessions, SessionInfo

sessions = discover_sessions(
    sessions_dir=None,        # Path, default ~/.claude/projects/
    project="myproject",      # Filter by project slug substring
    projects=["repo1","repo2"],  # Multi-project filter (any match)
    project_group="/home/user/repos",  # All projects under this path
    since="2026-04-01",       # Filter by date
    include_worktrees=False,  # Exclude worktree sessions
    min_size_bytes=10240,     # Skip trivial sessions
)
# Returns list[SessionInfo] with: session_id, project_slug, file_path, size_bytes, mtime
```

### Session Correlation

```python
from afterburn.scanner import group_sessions_by_parent

groups = group_sessions_by_parent(sessions)
# Returns: {parent_slug: {"parent_sessions": [...], "child_sessions": [...]}}
# Worktree sessions (agent dispatches) are linked to their parent orchestrator session
```

### Discovery

```python
from afterburn.discover import run_discover
# Accepts argparse namespace with: analysis_pass, since, project, projects,
# project_group, sessions_dir, format, include_worktrees, max_calls, max_tokens
# Passes: friction, patterns, gaps, releases
# Writes to .afterburn/: fix-list.md, pattern-catalog.md, skill-candidates/
```

### Findings

```python
from afterburn.findings import Finding, SkillCandidate, write_findings

finding = Finding(
    type="friction",          # friction | pattern | gap
    description="User correction: wrong approach",
    confidence=0.85,          # 0.0-1.0
    frequency=12,             # occurrence count
    sessions=["abc123"],      # session IDs
    evidence="User said: 'no, not that way'",
    verification="grep -r 'old_pattern' src/",
    theme="correction:process",   # taxonomy-prefixed themes: correction:{type}
)

write_findings([finding], Path(".afterburn"), fmt="markdown")  # or "json"
```

### Correction Taxonomy

```python
from afterburn.passes import classify_correction, suggest_remediation
from collections import Counter

# Classify a user correction into a taxonomy type
taxonomy = classify_correction("why did you push without testing?")
# Returns: "process" | "accuracy" | "scope" | "tooling" | "missing" | "unclassified"

# Generate targeted remediation suggestions
counts = Counter({"process": 15, "scope": 8, "missing": 3})
suggestions = suggest_remediation(counts)
# Returns: ["Add pre-commit hooks or workflow checklists",
#           "Add scope constraints: 'Answer in N sentences max'",
#           "Add completion checklists to skills"]
```

### Dead Release Detection

```python
from afterburn.dead_releases import detect_dead_releases

findings = detect_dead_releases("/path/to/repo")
# Scans git tags, finds new functions/classes per release,
# checks if they have callers in the codebase.
# Returns Finding objects for unwired code shipped in a release.
```

### Narrative

```python
from afterburn.narrative import run_narrative
# Accepts argparse namespace with: today, week, month, since, project, projects,
# project_group, sessions_dir, no_llm
# Writes .afterburn/narrative.md with stats + correction breakdown + LLM-generated narrative
```

### Analysis Passes

```python
from afterburn.passes import run_friction_pass, run_patterns_pass, run_gaps_pass

# All accept: sessions: list[SessionInfo], max_sessions: int
# Return: list[Finding]

findings = run_friction_pass(sessions, max_sessions=200)
# Extracts: user corrections (with taxonomy classification), tool denials, error patterns
# Sessions >= 10MB analyzed via RLM REPL
# Corrections sub-classified into: process, accuracy, scope, tooling, missing

findings = run_patterns_pass(sessions, max_sessions=200)
# Extracts: confirmed approaches, tool sequence patterns

findings = run_gaps_pass(sessions, max_sessions=100)
# Extracts: skill usage distribution, repeated manual workflows
```

### RLM REPL Engine

```python
from afterburn.vendor.rlm_repl import RLM_REPL

rlm = RLM_REPL(
    root_model="auto",         # or "haiku", model name
    recursive_model="auto",    # cheaper model for chunk analysis
    root_api_url="",           # AFTERBURN_API_URL or auto-detect
    max_iterations=999,        # no artificial cap
    verbose=True,
)

result = rlm.completion(
    context=any_python_object, # loaded into REPL as `context` variable
    query="Analyze this data and find patterns",
    system_prompt="",          # optional override
)
# Returns: string (the FINAL() answer from the REPL loop)

rlm.cost_summary()
# Returns: {"root": {calls, tokens}, "recursive": {calls, tokens}}
```

### LLM Clients

```python
# OpenAI-compatible API
from afterburn.vendor.rlm_repl.llm_client import LLMClient
client = LLMClient(api_url="http://localhost:8000/v1", model="auto")
response = client.chat([{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}])

# Claude Code CLI
from afterburn.vendor.rlm_repl.llm_client import ClaudeCLIClient
client = ClaudeCLIClient(model="haiku")
response = client.chat([{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}])

# Auto-detect best backend
from afterburn.vendor.rlm_repl.llm_client import _detect_backend
backend = _detect_backend()  # Returns "api" or "claude"
```

### Archive

```python
from afterburn.archive import run_archive
# Accepts argparse namespace with: days, sessions_dir, cwd, dry_run
# Archives sessions older than N days to .tgz, cleans history.jsonl
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Purpose |
|----------|---------|---------|
| AFTERBURN_API_URL | (auto-detect) | OpenAI-compatible API endpoint |
| AFTERBURN_NO_SSL_VERIFY | (unset) | Set to "1" for self-signed certs |
| ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | (unset) | For Claude API (if not using CLI) |
| OPENAI_API_KEY | (unset) | For OpenAI-compatible endpoints |

## Output Files

All written to `.afterburn/` in the working directory:

| File | Format | Content |
|------|--------|---------|
| fix-list.md | Markdown | Recurring friction with frequency and evidence |
| fix-list.jsonl | JSONL (--format json) | Machine-readable findings |
| pattern-catalog.md | Markdown | Successful patterns with confidence scores |
| skill-candidates/ | Directory of .md | Draft SKILL.md files |
| narrative.md | Markdown | Narrative report (from `afterburn narrative`) |
| provenance.json | JSON | Analysis metadata (sessions, timestamp, passes) |
| state.json | JSON | Incremental analysis state |
| errors.log | Text | Processing errors |

## Finding Schema (JSON)

```json
{
  "type": "friction|pattern|gap",
  "description": "string",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "frequency": 12,
  "sessions": ["session-uuid-1", "session-uuid-2"],
  "evidence": "string (max 500 chars)",
  "verification": "shell command or null",
  "theme": "wrong_approach|tool_denial|tool_error|..."
}
```

## Benchmarks

Tested on 93MB session (6,788 messages):

| Model | Backend | Iterations | Time | Findings | Precision |
|-------|---------|-----------|------|----------|-----------|
| qwen3-coder | vLLM (fp8) | 22 | 2m 15s | 5 | High |
| Claude Haiku | claude -p | 2 | 34s | 9 | Medium (some noise) |

Model card: https://servingcard.dev/model/qwen3-coder

## Dependencies

Runtime: Python 3.10+, requests
Optional: jq (for CCAR scripts)
Vendored: RLM engine (from arXiv:2512.24601, MIT), CCAR scripts (mitkox/ccar, MIT)

## Provenance

- Paper: "Recursive Language Models" — Zhang, Kraska, Khattab (arXiv:2512.24601)
- CCAR: https://github.com/mitkox/ccar (MIT)
- RLM reference: https://github.com/alexzhang13/rlm-minimal (MIT)
- Inspired by: DSPy GEPA optimizer, NousResearch hermes-agent-self-evolution
